note: Do not instantiate directly; use itasca.structure.link module functions.

method_groups.model: model, model_area, model_compression, model_disp, model_force, model_gap, model_gap_neg, model_gap_pos, model_stiffness, model_tension, model_yield, set_model_area, set_model_compression, set_model_gap, set_model_stiffness, set_model_tension

methods.name: attach
methods.description: Get the attached condition used by the link in each degree-of-freedom. The return value will be an integer with one of three values: 1 = free; 2 = rigid; and 3 = deformable. If the condition is deformable, see the \(model\) method (and associated methods) for details.

methods.name: component_id
methods.description: Get the Link component id.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete this structural link.

methods.name: extra
methods.description: Get the Link extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: group
methods.description: Get the Link group name in a given slot.

methods.name: group_remove
methods.signature: link.group_remove(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Remove from the given group from all group slots of the Link. One argument of type string, giving the group name, is required. The return value is a bool which is True if the group was removed from any slot, otherwise False.

methods.name: groups
methods.description: Get a dictionary describing which groups this Link is part of. The keys of the dictionary are the slot names and the values are the group names.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Get the Link structural element id.

methods.name: in_group
methods.signature: link.in_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Test if the Link is part of a given group. If the optional argument slot is given, only that slot is searched. Otherwise, all group slots are searched.

methods.name: model
methods.description: Get the name of the 1-D force-displacement model used in each degree-of-freedom. If the link attached condition is free or rigid, then this function will return None (see the \(attach\) method). The currently available models are \(linear\), \(normal-yield\), \(shear-yield\), and \(pile-yield\).

methods.name: model_area
methods.description: Get the area associated with all degrees-of-freedom in the force-displacement model.

methods.name: model_compression
methods.description: Get the compressive yield force associated with the force-displacement model for all degrees-of-freedom.

methods.name: model_disp
methods.description: Get the relative displacement between the node and the target used by each 1-D force-displacement model. Note that displacements are with respect to the node-local coordinate system. The relative displacement is defined as the displacement of the link target minus the the displacement of the link host node in the node local coordinate system.

methods.name: model_force
methods.description: Get the current force in each 1-D force-displacement model. The force is calculated by the 1-D force-displacement model in an incremental fashion from the incremental relative displacement of the link target and the host node (target minus node). See the \(model_disp\) method.

methods.name: model_gap
methods.description: Get the flag indicating whether gap calculations are active in the 1-D force-displacement model for all degrees-of-freedom. See the \(gap_neg\) and \(gap_pos\) methods.

methods.name: model_gap_neg
methods.description: Get the negative (compressive) gap component of each 1-D force-displacement model for each degree-of-freedom.

methods.name: model_gap_pos
methods.description: Get the positive (tensile) gap component of each 1-D force-displacement model for each degree-of-freedom. This value will only affect force calculations if the gap-use flag is on; see the \(model_gap\) method. The relative displacement is defined as the displacement of the link target minus the displacement of the link host node in the node local coordinate system. See the \(model_disp\) method.

methods.name: model_stiffness
methods.description: Get the stiffness used by each 1-D force-displacement model for each degree-of-freedom.

methods.name: model_tension
methods.description: Get the tensile yield force associated with the force-displacement model for each degree-of-freedom. The tensile yield force is a limit to how positive the force in the link can be. Note that tension is defined relative to the node-local coordinate system, and, thus, depending on the coordinate systems involved, the definition of compression and tension may not match those used by the element connected to the host node. See the \(model_force\) method.

methods.name: model_yield
methods.description: Get the yield state indicator in the 1-D force displacement model each degree-of-freedom. This is returned as an integer where 0 = never yielded, 1 = yielding now, and 2 = yielded in past.

methods.name: node
methods.description: Get the node hosting the structural link.

methods.name: set_extra
methods.description: Set the Link extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: set_group
methods.signature: link.set_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> None
methods.description: Set the Link group name in a given slot.

methods.name: set_model_area
methods.description: Set the area associated with one component of the degrees-of-freedom. The integer component starts at zero and the translational components are first.

methods.name: set_model_compression
methods.description: Set the compressive yield force associated with the force-displacement model for a single degree-of-freedom. The integer component starts at zero and the translational components are first.

methods.name: set_model_gap
methods.description: Set the flag indicating whether gap calculations are active in the 1-D force-displacement model for a single degrees-of-freedom. See the \(gap_neg\) and \(gap_pos\) methods.

methods.name: set_model_stiffness
methods.description: Set the stiffness used by each 1-D force-displacement model for each degree-of-freedom.

methods.name: set_model_tension
methods.description: Set the tensile yield force associated with the force-displacement model for a singe degree-of-freedom. The tensile yield force is a limit to how positive the force in the link can be. Note that tension is defined relative to the node-local coordinate system, and, thus, depending on the coordinate systems involved, the definition of compression and tension may not match those used by the element connected to the host node. See the \(model_force\) method. The integer component starts at zero and the translational components are first.

methods.name: set_slide
methods.description: Set the large-strain sliding flag for the link. This function only applies to node-to-zone links; it has no meaning for node-to-node links.

methods.name: set_slide_tol
methods.description: Get the large-strain sliding tolerance for the link. This function only applies to node-to-zone links; it has no meaning for node-to-node links.

methods.name: side
methods.description: Get the side number of the link. Unless the host node of the link is attached to an embedded liner, this will always return 1. If the host node is part of an embedded liner, it may be 2, indicating the link is being used on side 2 of the liner.

methods.name: slide
methods.description: Get the large-strain sliding flag for the link. This function only applies to node-to-zone links; it has no meaning for node-to-node links.

methods.name: slide_tol
methods.description: Get the large-strain sliding tolerance for the link. This function only applies to node-to-zone links; it has no meaning for node-to-node links.

methods.name: target
methods.description: Get the target of this link. The return value is a Node object for a node-node link and a Zone object for a node-zone link.

methods.name: type
methods.description: Get the name of the link target type. A node-to-node link will return an \(itasca.structure.node.Node\) object. A node-to-zone link will return an \(itasca.zone.Zone\) object.

methods.name: used_by
methods.description: Get the name of the type of structural element with which this link is associated. This only applies to node-to-zone links, otherwise the return is a null string. The type string will be one of \(beam\), \(cable\), \(pile\), \(shell\), \(geogrid\), or \(liner\). If the attach conditions and models assigned to the link are not compatible with those elements (generally because they have been changed), then an empty string is returned.

methods.name: valid
methods.description: Returns True if this Link is live.
